title: License + Ethics + Dual-use triage checklist (blocking gate)
context: Every candidate protocol source must pass a mandatory License, Ethics, and Dual-use screen before authoring begins; no protocol may enter the pipeline without this gate passing. The gate must enumerate accepted licenses (CC0, CC-BY) versus cite-only (CC-BY-NC, copyrighted journals), encode ethics checks for human-derived material (IRB/consent) and animal use (IACUC/3Rs), and enforce a dual-use-of-concern screen. Without a published, ratified gate checklist, the project has no enforceable baseline for license compliance, ethics, or DURC exclusion.
objective: Design, write, and publish the License + Ethics + Dual-use triage checklist that serves as the blocking gate every candidate protocol source must pass before authoring, capturing license evidence, ethics flags, and dual-use screening criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist enumerates accepted licenses (CC0, CC-BY) versus cite-only (CC-BY-NC, all copyrighted: Nature/CSH/Springer Protocols, Methods Mol Biol); default is EXCLUDE on unverifiable license.
License PASS requires recorded license id + URL + text snapshot (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback URL) and a permitsDerivatives flag derived from a cited clause.
Ethics section flags human-derived material (IRB/consent required) and animal use (IACUC/3Rs required); forbids patient/identifiable/controlled-access data.
Dual-use screen rejects DURC/weaponizable methods (viral-vector/gain-of-function/select-agent-adjacent); reviewer identity recorded for each decision.
Document committed to the project repo with DCO sign-off; designated License+Ethics gate owner recorded.
resources: NIH Dual Use Research of Concern (DURC) policy — https://www.phe.gov/s3/dualuse/
Creative Commons license compatibility chart — https://creativecommons.org/licenses/
Wayback Machine CDX API for snapshot evidence — https://archive.org/help/wayback_api.php
output: A committed checklist document (License+Ethics+Dual-use gate) covering license evidence, ethics flags, and DURC screening, with a designated gate-owner role and a ratified decision that no protocol authoring starts without a PASS.
